prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetheart, think of the computer like a busy kitchen, and the CPU is the head chef standing right at the center of it all. All of your games and pictures are like recipes, and this little chip is the one reading each step and doing the most important mixing and measuring. It can't remember everything on its own, so it keeps sending helpers to the cupboards and the fridge—that’s the computer’s memory—to fetch what it needs. It works incredibly fast, making millions of little decisions every second, so you can watch your cartoons or draw your pictures without a wait. It’s truly the tiny, hard-working brain of the whole operation.
